title: Tableau Token Expiration Check and Auto-Renewal
description:
Implements automatic token expiration checking for Tableau API authentication. Tableau Personal Access Tokens expire after 7 days. Before making any API request to Tableau, the system now checks if the stored token is still valid. If expired, a new token is automatically regenerated using the stored credentials without requiring user intervention.

This ensures a seamless experience when users click the Import from Tableau button — the token renewal is transparent.
acceptance_criteria: System checks token expiration before each Tableau API request
Expired tokens are automatically regenerated using stored credentials
Token renewal is transparent to the user with no additional prompts
Valid tokens within the 7-day window are reused without regeneration
middleware_changes: Added token expiration check logic before Tableau API calls. If the stored token is older than 7 days, the system automatically re-authenticates using the stored PAT credentials and replaces the token.
implementation_notes: Tableau PATs expire after 7 days. The auto-renewal triggers only when the user initiates a Tableau action (e.g., Import from Tableau). No background renewal process.
comment_summary: Waqar implemented and confirmed that token auto-regeneration triggers only when the user clicks Import from Tableau and the existing token has expired.
